item.title: 我作为软件工程师第一年的反思 | A Reflection of My First Year as a Software Engineer
item.description:
十二个月前，这是我作为软件工程师的第一天，这是我从编码训练营毕业后的第一份技术工作。 以前我在酒店待了大约八年，所以老实说，每当我意识到这一点时，我仍然觉得自己难以置信。 我真的不知道会发生什么。 一切都是新的，工作文化、我的同事和工作本身。 我急切地踏入这个新世界，心中有一件确定的事情，我能给自己的最好机会就是尽我所能，同时保持真实的自己。 在短短的 12 个月里，我体会到了在享受我为之工作的生活的同时享受我所做的工作意味着什么。 在那一年里充满了挑战、学习和许多值得感激的东西。 我希望为那些正在寻求过渡到科技行业的人、刚刚开始他们的技术职业生涯的人或任何可能从我的经验中发现价值的人提供见解。 这是我作为软件工程师一年的反映。

我面临的最大挑战之一是导航调整。 在酒店工作，我习惯了来上班，做工作，第二天再做。 这听起来像是我现在所做的那种工作应该是正常的一天，但有一段时间并非如此。 在我以前的工作中，大部分工作是体力劳动，现在我所做的所有工作都在头脑中。 我从 8 多小时的四处走动变成了 8 多小时的久坐不动的工作，凝视着太空，不断地计算和解决问题。 最大的调整是找到一种方法来关闭我的思想并将工作与我的个人生活分开。 我发现自己一直在思考，就好像我的思想有自己的思想一样。 即使在我不应该再这样的时候，比如睡觉。 有几个晚上我难以入睡，因为我的大脑要么还在计算如何构建一个功能，要么在回溯一个 bug 是如何产生的。 我也犯了一个错误，就是盯着同一个代码看几个小时来想出一个解决方案。 在它发生之前，我没有意识到精神上的损失。 也许，我也否认你会厌倦做你喜欢做的事情。 老实说，我花了一些时间才真正理解这一点。 我很兴奋，渴望，也有动力，我在不知不觉中把自己逼到了精疲力竭的地步。 这是我直到发生在我身上才完全理解的事情之一。 我最终找到了解决方案。 我通过调整向我的大脑发送信息的某些场景和空间来帮助自己，是时候放慢速度了。 当我不在工作区的周边，或者只是在看 Netflix 时，有几个例子是和家人一起出去玩的。 尽管我取得了一些进展，但我知道这仍在进行中。 我看到实际上在精神上休息的重要性。 心理健康日的价值变得显而易见。

对我来说突出的下一个挑战是冒名顶替者偶尔不请自来的访问。 被聪明的人包围是令人振奋的，但有时我会发现自己不知不觉地贬低了自己的价值，尤其是在我觉得自己什么都没做的时候。 这些天要么碰壁，试图以猎豹般的速度学习多种技术及其复杂性，要​​么发现自己陷入了兔子洞，完全迷失了方向，不知道下一步该做什么。 尽管我不喜欢那些日子，但那些日子是我在团队内外提出问题的关键。 它把我带到了合作的大门。 我认为浪费在没有产生任何东西上的时间对于形成表明我自己的研究和发现的问题至关重要。 冒名顶替者喜欢把我挑出来，好像我所做的工作只能由我一个人完成，如果我不知道该怎么做，那么我不属于这里。 它还引导我进行沟通，让自己容易受到同事和导师的影响。 通过这个，我也能够建立关系并建立信任。 最后因为冒名顶替者，我开始写我的开发日志。 它们是我的高点，我的低点，以及我从高点和低点评估的不断发展的目标。 它帮助我看到了自己的进步，并为冒名顶替者提供了证据，表明是时候离开了。 当访问发生时，可能很难清楚地看到和思考，但每次，我都会变得更强大和更聪明。 我承认冒名顶替者不会消失。 我不再试图阻止它进来，而是张开双臂拥抱它，准备从挑战中学习一些东西。

虽然我所经历的这些挑战是我自己的，但我必须说我不是一个人经历的。 我在家里有一个强大的支持系统，但当支持直接来自工作时，它的影响就更大了。 我保持真实身份的一种方式是能够与我的经理进行透明的沟通。 我很感激当被问到“你好吗？”时我能够完全诚实。 不用担心脆弱会被评判和看不起。 我认为我的经理们并没有完全理解这些 1:1 对我的旅程有多大帮助。 我感谢我们整个组织的协作性质，尤其是在结对编程或调试方面向其他团队寻求帮助时。 他们从一天中抽出的一小时不仅对解决问题很有影响，而且对处理冒名顶替者也有影响。 当我需要喘口气和休息时，我可以享受无限的病假和心理健康日，我对此心存感激。 我很感激工作文化和工作活动，让我能够结识团队之外的同事并结交朋友。 很明显，我很感激能在 Volley 工作，这是我的第一份技术工作。 也许对某些人来说，所有这些东西都是每个公司都应该拥有的，但所有这些对我来说都是新的和陌生的。 在这一年中，我通过我的工作和同事的努力克服了这些挑战，但让我脚踏实地、勇往直前的是感激之情。

时不时地，当我需要增强信心时，我会浏览我的开发日志。 这证明了我有时无法认识到的稳定增长。 我从结对编程和编写小功能开始，并且绝对在协作中找到了这种乐趣。 在接下来的几个月里，我开始构建比以前更复杂的功能。 就在最近，我们为我们的产品 Backchannel 推出了一种新的语音用户体验，它由我和我才华横溢的队友 Will 共同编写。 我曾经害怕创建拉取请求（代码更改），更不用说阅读组织的内部插件的代码库了。 在过去的一年里，我做了上游的改变，我贡献了 8 个！ 在我们的团队会议上，我以前很安静，现在我自信地不仅问“如何”，还问“为什么”。 我从不知道如何以及在哪里寻找错误到在调试时开发系统。 我曾经害怕部署，现在我每周至少部署一次。 最后，就在几个月前，我有机会根据自己作为初级工程师的经验为我们自己的学徒计划做出贡献。 话虽如此，我也开始指导一位像我一样进行职业转型的初级工程师。 我确实偶尔会怀疑“如果怎么办？” 在我的脑海里，因为我知道还有很多我不知道的事情。 然而，每次我们结对编程并且我能够教我的学员一些东西本身就是验证。 这些任务中的每一项都曾经令人生畏，但随着时间和实践的推移，它们会慢慢消失，并且绝对是有效和有益的。

我敬畏。 回顾过去的十二个月，我不禁感到自豪。 我在精神上、情感上和技术上都学到了很多东西。 这些段落不足以彻底描述我所经历的经历。 我意识到我更多地触及了工作的心理和情感方面，而不是技术部分。 我所做工作的核心是保持清晰和开放的心态。 如果我的思想被这些挑战占据，我将无法尽我所能做好我的工作。 我了解了工作与生活平衡的意义和意义，并知道明天工作将永远存在。 我学会了欢迎冒名顶替者，因为它只会让我更加坚持。 我学会了耐心，因为在我的工具带上增加技能自然需要时间。 我在前几个月试图学习的技术，在过去的十二个月里我稳步学习和实施。 我对拼图如何组合在一起有了更好的理解。 学习我们使用的工具和技术总是具有挑战性的，但只要我能够解决这一过程中的挑战，它就不应该阻碍我不断学习。 过去一年的经验使我成为了今天的软件工程师。 我真的很高兴我克服了疑虑，并相信我比我认为自己的能力要强。 我期待在不断变化的环境中拥抱未知，因为无论结果如何，总有一些东西需要学习。



原文:

Twelve months ago, it was my first day as a Software Engineer at my very first tech job since graduating from a coding boot camp. Previously I was in hospitality for about eight years, so honestly, I still find myself in disbelief whenever this dawns on me. I didn’t really know what to expect. Everything was new, the work culture, my colleagues, and the work itself. I eagerly stepped into this new world with one thing certain on my mind, the best chance I could give myself is by doing the best that I could while staying genuine to who I am. In the short twelve months, I’ve gotten a taste of what it means to be enjoying the work that I do while living the life I’m working for. And packed in that one year were challenges, learnings, and a lot to be grateful for. I’m hoping to give insight to people who are looking into transitioning into the tech industry, who are just starting their technical careers, or to anyone who may find value in my experience. This is a reflection of my one year as a software engineer.

One of the biggest challenges I’ve faced was navigating adjustments. Working in hospitality, I was used to coming to work, doing the work, and doing it again the next day. It sounds like how a normal day should be in the kind of work that I do now but for some time it wasn’t. A major part of the labor in my old job was physical and now all of the work that I do now is in the mind. I went from 8+ hours of moving around to 8+ hours of sedentary work staring into space, constantly calculating and problem-solving. The big adjustment was finding a way to turn off my mind and separate work from my personal life. I find myself thinking all the time as if my mind has a mind of its own. Even at times when I shouldn’t be anymore, like sleeping. There were nights when I struggled to fall asleep because my mind was either still computing how to build a feature or retracing the steps of how a bug was produced. I’ve also made the mistake of trying to crank out a solution by staring at the same code for hours. I was unaware of the mental toll until it was happening. Maybe, I was also in denial that you could get tired of doing something you enjoy doing. Honestly, it took me some time to really understand this. I was excited, eager, and motivated, that I drove myself into burning out without even knowing it. It was one of those things I didn’t fully understand until it happened to me. I eventually came to a solution. I helped myself by aligning certain scenarios and spaces that send a message to my brain, it’s time to slow down. A couple of examples are hanging out with family, when I’m not around the perimeter of my workspace, or just watching Netflix. Although I made some strides, I’m aware it's still a work in progress. I see the importance of actually, mentally, taking a break. The value of mental health days became apparent.

The next challenge that stood out to me is the occasional uninvited visit from the imposter. It’s invigorating to be surrounded by smart people but sometimes I find myself unconsciously discounting my own value, especially on days when I felt like I didn’t produce anything. These days are either when I have hit a wall, trying to learn multiple technologies and their intricacies at cheetah-like speed, or have found myself in a rabbit hole, completely lost, unsure of what the next step should be. As much as I don’t like those days, those days were the key to me reaching out and asking questions inside and outside of my team. It led me to the door of collaboration. The time spent I thought was wasted by producing nothing was crucial in forming questions that indicate my own research and findings. The imposter likes to single me out as if the work that I do can only be done solely by me and if I don’t know how to do it then I don’t belong here. It also led me to communicate and allow myself to be vulnerable to my colleagues and my mentors. Through this, I was also able to form relationships and build trust. Lastly because of the imposter, I started writing my dev journal. They were my highs, my lows, and my evolving goals assessed from the highs and lows. It helped me see my progress and provided evidence to the imposter that it was time to depart. It can be difficult to see and think clearly when the visits happen, but each time, I just come out of it stronger and wiser. I accepted that the imposter doesn’t go away. I stopped trying to prevent it from coming in, instead, I embrace it with open arms, ready to learn something from the challenge.

Although these challenges I went through were my own, I must say that I didn’t go through them alone. I had a strong support system at home, but it was an even stronger impact when support was coming directly from work. One of my ways of staying genuine to who I am is being able to communicate transparently with my managers. I’m grateful that I’m able to be completely honest when asked “How are you doing?” without worrying that vulnerability will be judged and looked down upon. I don’t think my managers grasp the entirety of how helpful those 1:1s have been to my journey. I’m grateful for the collaborating nature of our whole organization, especially when asking other teams for help in terms of pair programming or debugging. The one hour they’ve taken out of their day has been impactful not only in problem-solving but also in dealing with the imposter. I’m grateful for the unlimited sick and mental health days I could take when I need to take a breath and take a break. I’m grateful for the work culture and work events which allowed me to get to know and make friends with colleagues who are outside my team. It's probably obvious that I am grateful to be working at Volley as my very first tech job. Maybe to some people, all these things are something every company should have by default, but all of these were new and unfamiliar to me. Throughout this year, I surpassed these challenges through a culmination of my work and my colleagues, but what kept me grounded and going above all else is gratitude.

Every now and then when I need a boost of confidence, I go through my dev journal. It’s a testament to the steady growth that I sometimes fail to recognize. I started off pair programming and writing small features and absolutely found such joy in collaborating. Over the next couple of months, I started building more complex features than the previous. And just recently we pushed a new voice user experience of our product, Backchannel, written in tandem by myself and my awesomely talented teammate, Will. I used to be intimidated creating pull requests (code changes), let alone reading the organization’s internal plugins’ codebase. In the past year, I’ve made upstream changes and I’ve contributed to 8 of them! I used to be quiet in our team meetings and now I’m confidently asking not just the “Hows” but also the “Whys”. I went from not knowing how and where to look for bugs to developing a system when debugging. I used to be afraid to deploy and now I’m deploying at least once a week. And lastly, just a couple of months ago I had the opportunity to contribute to our own apprentice program based on my own experience as a junior engineer. With that said, I also started mentoring a junior engineer who also did a career transition like me. I do get the occasional self-doubt of “what ifs?” in my head, because I know there are a lot of things I still don’t know. However, every time that we pair program and I was able to teach my mentee something is validation on its own. Each of these tasks was once daunting but with time and practice that slowly faded away and has been absolutely validating and rewarding.

I am in awe. Looking back on the last twelve months, I can’t help but feel a sense of pride. I have learned so much mentally, emotionally, and technically. These paragraphs are not nearly enough to thoroughly describe the experience I went through. I realized that I touched more on the mental and emotional aspects of the job than the technical parts. The very core of the work that I do is having a clear and open mind. If my mind is occupied with these challenges, I won’t be able to do my job at my best. I learned the meaning and significance of work-life balance and know that work will always be there tomorrow. I learned to welcome the imposter because it has only made me persevere even more. I learned to be patient because adding skills to my tool belt naturally just takes time. The technologies I was trying to cram in the first couple of months, I steadily learned and implemented in the last twelve months. I have a better understanding of how the puzzle pieces fit together. Learning the tools and technologies we use is always challenging, but as long as I address the challenges along the way, it should not hinder me from continuously learning. My experience from the past year has made me the software engineer that I am today. I am genuinely happy that I pushed through the doubts and believed that I was more than what I thought I was capable of. I look forward to embracing the unknown in an environment of constant change because no matter the outcome, there’s always something to learn.
item.author: gamil91

item.title: 使用 AWS Lambda 函数 URL 将消息发布到 Slack | Post Messages To Slack Using AWS Lambda Function URLs
item.description:
若苏埃·布斯托斯

发表于本教程将向您展示如何使用其传入 Webhook API 和 AWS Lambda 函数 URL 将消息发布到 Slack 应用程序。

什么是 AWS Lambda 函数 URL？

函数 URL 是 Lambda 函数的专用 HTTP(S) 端点。 您可以通过 Lambda 控制台或 Lambda API 创建和配置函数 URL。 当您创建函数 URL 时，Lambda 会自动为您生成唯一的 URL 端点。 在此处了解有关 AWS 函数 URL 的更多信息

什么是 Slack 传入 Webhook？

传入 Webhook 是一种将消息从应用程序发布到 Slack 的简单方法。 创建传入 Webhook 会为您提供一个唯一的 URL，您可以将带有消息文本和一些选项的 JSON 有效负载发送到该 URL。 您可以将所有常见的格式和布局块与 Incoming Webhooks 一起使用，以使消息脱颖而出。 在此处了解有关 Slack 传入 Webhook 的更多信息

先决条件

要继续进行，您将需要以下内容：

  • 一个 AWS 账户（在这里创建一个）
  • 已安装并配置 AWS CLI。 在此处设置说明
  • 一个 Slack 帐户（在此处创建一个）
  • 无需 GUI 即可轻松执行终端命令。
  • 舒适地使用和调试 Node.js 和 JavaScript。

像这样一个AWS CDK Node.JS：Hello World

好的，让我们开始吧！

创建 Slack 应用程序频道

首先，您要预先确定用于接收传入消息的 Slack 应用程序通道。 例如，您可以使用现有频道（如 #random）或创建新频道。 对于此示例，我将使用 #my-test-app 命名约定。 请参阅下图以供参考。

创建一个 Slack 应用程序

要从外部应用程序接收消息，例如从您的网站或移动应用程序，您必须配置您的 Slack“API 应用程序”设置。

登录您的 Slack 帐户并导航 Slack API 门户“您的应用程序”页面。 复制粘贴此链接以立即跳转： https ://api.slack.com/apps。

如果这是您第一次创建 Slack 帐户，您的应用程序列表将为空。 否则，您将看到您的 API 应用程序列表。

接下来，单击创建新应用程序或创建应用程序绿色按钮以打开“创建应用程序”模式窗口。

选择从头开始选项。

在App Name中，输入您的应用名称。 我在这个例子中使用“Send Me Tacos”，但你可以选择任何你喜欢的东西。 接下来，使用下拉菜单找到要发送消息的工作区。 工作区是您创建的帐户的名称。 在此示例中，我将其命名为“Taco Paradise”。

你能说我在想美味的食物吗？

完成后，单击创建应用程序。 最后一步将带您进入新页面。 请参阅图片以供参考。

创建新应用后，留在同一页面并向下滚动以在左侧菜单列表中找到Incoming Webhooks链接。 单击链接以显示 webhook 配置页面。 请注意“激活传入 Webhook”旁边的文字是如何设置为关闭的。

单击该开关以启用它。 它应该变成绿色并显示文本“开”。

做得好！

创建一个 Webhook

最后，单击Add New Webhook to Workspace按钮以创建 webhook URL。 此步骤将打开一个新窗口。

在新窗口中，注意它显示“测试应用程序需要一个频道作为应用程序发布到”的位置，单击下拉菜单并选择您要发送消息的频道。

注意：我们在教程开始时引用了此步骤。 在此示例中，我选择了#my-test-app。

您现在可以试用新的 webhook URL。 使用您首选的终端应用程序或 API 客户端，将您的示例 curl 请求复制粘贴到 API 客户端或终端并执行命令。

注意：将 webhook URL 替换为您的 URL。

 curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' --data '{"text":"Hello, World!"}' https://hooks.slack.com/services/5CKKM62NX5J/8G8XMGQ3RXM/aadCZ8act32sLJ7dgaC8HLrx

执行命令后，导航回 Slack 应用程序、Web 或移动设备并注意新消息； 多田！
非常酷！！！

您可能不会立即注意到它，但发生了两件事。 很酷的想法💡：您可以使用 Postman 和 Insomnia API 客户端来测试和原型化您的 Slack webhook 和 AWS 函数 URL。 您可以在这里停下来收工，但如果您想灵活使用，请继续下一节。

创建 AWS Lambda 函数 URL

就在那时！

让我们将本教程提升到一个新的水平。 在下一部分中，我将向您展示如何创建和配置 AWS Lambda 函数 URL，以便您可以使用 Slack Incoming Webhooks。😃

此外，我们将使用 AWS 命令​​行界面创建称为角色和策略的 AWS 身份权限，以允许函数 URL 工作。

如果您需要复习，请在此处访问 AWS IAM 文档。

准备好！？ 我们走吧！

创建 AWS IAM 角色

首先，我们需要访问您的 ARN，它是 AWS 特定的格式化字符串。 类似于这个：

要访问它，我们可以在终端中执行以下 AWS CLI 命令，如下所示：

注意：将 Paulo 更改为您的用户名

$ aws iam get-user --user-name Paulo

响应应该类似于下面的 JSON 文本。 将您的文本保存在某处以供以后参考，或者您可以重新运行该命令。

 $ { "User" : { "UserName" : "Paulo" , "Path" : "/" , "CreateDate" : "2019-09-21T23:03:13Z" , "UserId" : "AIDA123456789EXAMPLE" , "Arn" : "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/Paulo" } }

将 AWS 策略附加到 IAM 角色

接下来，创建一个名为trust-policy.json的文件

// trust-policy.json
{ "Version" : "2012-10-17" , "Statement" : [ { "Effect" : "Allow" , "Principal" : { "Service" : "lambda.amazonaws.com" }, "Action" : "sts:AssumeRole" } ]
}

在这里暂停并将角色名称function-url-role更改为其他名称或保留它，直到您创建另一个。

准备好后，打开终端或命令提示符，复制粘贴下面的命令，然后按 Enter。

 $ aws iam create-role --role-name function -url-role --assume-role-policy-document file://trust-policy.json

成功的响应将返回更多 JSON 文本。

下一步会将 AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole 策略附加到您新创建的 IAM 角色。 此策略为您提供运行函数的基本用户权限。

继续复制粘贴并在您的终端应用程序中执行以下命令。

请记住将您的功能角色名称替换为您的名称。 在这个例子中它的function-url-role 。

 $ aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name function -url-role --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole

创建 AWS 函数 URL

在本节中，您将创建和配置您的 AWS Lambda 函数，并使用一些基本设置启用新的函数 URL 功能。

首先，我们需要访问 ARN 角色字符串。 为此，请执行下面的 AWS CLI 命令。

 $ aws iam get-role --role-name function -url-role

响应输出应返回一些类似于以下文本的 JSON。

暂停一秒钟并找到类似于此文本arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/my-function-url的“Arn”字符串值并将其保存在某个地方以备后用。

 // terminal JSON ouput
{ "Role" : { "Path" : "/" , "RoleName" : "my-function-url" , "RoleId" : "AIDA123456789EXAMPLE" , "Arn" : "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/my-function-url" , "CreateDate" : "2022-04-16T22:32:36+00:00" , "AssumeRolePolicyDocument" : { "Version" : "2012-10-17" , "Statement" : [ { "Effect" : "Allow" , "Principal" : { "Service" : "lambda.amazonaws.com" }, "Action" : "sts:AssumeRole" } ] }, "Description" : "Allows Lambda functions to call AWS services on your behalf." , "MaxSessionDuration" : 3600 , "RoleLastUsed" : { "LastUsedDate" : "2022-04-30T00:29:38+00:00" , "Region" : "us-west-2" } }
}

准备你的文件

在创建 AWS Lambda 函数之前，您需要准备一些代码，执行一些 AWS CLI 命令，最后部署您的项目以进行实时测试。 对于此示例，我们将使用 JavaScript 来处理 Node.js。

首先，创建一个名为index.js的文件，然后准备要编辑的文件。 将以下命令与您首选的终端应用程序一起使用，或者您可以手动修改它。

 $ touch index.js && vim index.js

接下来，将以下 JavaScript 代码复制粘贴到终端的 vim 编辑会话中，或使用您喜欢的代码编辑器。 确保将<-- YOUR WEBHOOK GOES HERE -->的“字符串”替换为您的 Slack API webhook URL。

例如，从此：

对此

路径：'/services/T02S60H1F0Q/B03DH5L14S1/CPHv2eu0ctGgB​​9g99DNZiNCi'

// index.js const https = require ( ' https ' ); const postRequest = ( a ) => { const payload = { " text " : ` ${ a } ` }; const data = JSON . stringify ( payload ); const options = { hostname : ' hooks.slack.com ' , path : ' <-- YOUR WEBHOOK GOES HERE --> ' , port : 443 , method : ' POST ' , headers : { ' Content-Type ' : ' application/x-www-form-urlencoded; charset=UTF-8 ' , ' Content-Length ' : data . length } }; return new Promise (( resolve , reject ) => { const req = https . request ( options , res => { let rawData = '' ; res . on ( ' data ' , chunk => { rawData += chunk ; }); res . on ( ' end ' , () => { resolve ({ statusCode : 200 , body : ` ${ rawData } ` }); }); }); req . on ( ' error ' , err => { reject ( new Error ( err )); }); req . write ( data ); req . end (); }); }; exports . handler = async ( event ) => { let body = JSON . parse ( event . body ); const result = await postRequest ( body . text ); const response = { statusCode : 200 , body : result , }; return response ; };

最后，使用以下命令或手动压缩文件。

 $ zip function .zip index.js

您在以下部分中使用 AWS CLI 创建 AWS Lambda 函数。

创建 AWS Lambda 函数

在这里暂停一秒钟。 为你的新功能想一个名字。 在此示例中，我使用的是my-url-function 。

还记得您之前保存的 ARN 字符串吗？ 将 ARN 字符串替换为您的字符串。

例如，从此：

对此

准备好后，复制粘贴并在终端中执行以下命令：

 $ aws lambda create-function \ --function-name my-url-function \ --runtime nodejs14.x \ --zip-file fileb://function.zip \ --handler index.handler \ --role [ YOUR ARN GOES HERE]

输出应返回带有您的 AWS Lambda 函数详细信息的扩展 JSON 响应。

您可以在终端中使用以下命令来验证您的 lambda 函数是否已创建。

 $ awd lambda get-functionn --function-name my-url-function

启用 AWS 函数 URL

传统上，您必须经过十几个步骤才能使用 AWS Lambda、API 网关创建一个简单的终端节点，当然在创建安全终端节点时也是如此，但现在有了更好的方法。

执行以下命令，记得用你的函数名替换你的函数名。

由此：

对此：

注意：对于这个演示，链接是公开的。 您负责保护所有生产环境中的端点。

测试您的 AWS 函数 URL

现在，您一直在等待。 在终端中使用以下命令并执行。

 $ curl -X POST \ '<YOUR FUNCTION URL GOES HERE>' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -d '{"text": "Feed me Seymour!"}'

一个成功的响应应该返回一些类似于下面的 JSON 文本。

 $ { "body" : "ok" , "statusCode" :200 }

如果您成功了，您应该会在 Slack 应用程序频道中看到另一条消息，其中包含文本“喂我，西摩！”。

结论

就是这样！

我希望您提高了一些 AWS 云无服务器技能并喜欢本教程。

我很感激你能走到这一步，我希望下次再见！



原文:

Josue Bustos

Posted on May 1

This tutorial will show you how to post messages to Slack apps using their Incoming Webhook API and the AWS Lambda Function URL.

What is an AWS Lambda Function URL?

A function URL is a dedicated HTTP(S) endpoint for your Lambda function. You can create and configure a function URL through the Lambda console or the Lambda API. Lambda automatically generates a unique URL endpoint for you when you make a function URL. -- AWS docs.

Learn more about AWS Function URLS here

What is a Slack Incoming Webhook?

Incoming Webhooks are a simple way to post messages from apps into Slack. Creating an Incoming Webhook gives you a unique URL to which you send a JSON payload with the message text and some options. You can use all the usual formatting and layout blocks with Incoming Webhooks to make the messages stand out. -- Slack API Docs

Learn more about Slack Incoming Webhooks here

Prerequisites

To follow along, you will need the following:

  • A AWS account (create one here)
  • AWS CLI installed and configured. Set up instructions here
  • A Slack account (create one here)
  • AWS Lambda Function (fundamentals)
  • AWS IAM Roles, Policies (fundamentals).
  • Comfortable executing terminal commands without GUI.
  • Comfortable using and debugging Node.js and JavaScript.

Cool Idea 💡: In the future, you can use this example in an AWS SAM or AWS CDK project. Like this one AWS CDK Node.JS: Hello World

Okay, let's get started!

Create Slack App Channel

First, you want to predetermine which Slack app channel to use to receive incoming messages. For example, you can use an existing channel like #random or create a new channel. For this example, I will be using the #my-test-app naming convention. See the image below for reference.

Create a Slack App

To receive messages from external applications, say from your website or a mobile application, you must configure your Slack "API app" settings.

Sign in to your Slack account and navigate the Slack API portal "Your Apps" page. Copy-paste this link to jump there now: https://api.slack.com/apps.

If this is your first time creating a Slack account Your Apps list will be empty. Otherwise, you will see a list of your API apps.

Next, click on the Create New App or Create an App green button to open the "Create an app" modal window.

Select the From scratch option.

In the App Name, type your app name. I use "Send Me Tacos" for this example, but you can choose anything you like. :)

Next, use the dropdown menu to locate the workspace where you want to send messages. The workspace is the name of the account you created. In this example, I named it "Taco Paradise".

Can you tell I'm thinking about yummy food?

When done, click on Create App. This last step will take you to a new page. See the image for reference.

After creating your new app, stay on the same page and scroll down to locate the Incoming Webhooks link on the left menu list. Click on the link to display the webhook configuration page. Notice how next to the words "Activate Incoming Webhooks" is set to off.

Click on that switch to enable it. It should turn green and display the text "On."

Well done!

Create a Webhook

Finally, click on the Add New Webhook to Workspace button to create a webhook URL. This step opens a new window.

In the new window, notice where it says, "Test App requires a channel to post to as an app" click on the dropdown menu and select the channel you want to send your messages.

Note: We referenced this step at the beginning of the tutorial. In this example, I chose #my-test-app.

You can now take your new webhook URL for a spin. Using your preferred terminal application or API client, Copy-paste YOUR sample curl request into the API client or terminal and execute the command/s.

Note: Replace the webhook URL with yours.

curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' --data '{"text":"Hello, World!"}' https://hooks.slack.com/services/5CKKM62NX5J/8G8XMGQ3RXM/aadCZ8act32sLJ7dgaC8HLrx

After executing the command, navigate back to the Slack app, web, or mobile and notice the new message; "Hello, World!".

Tada!
SO COOL!!!

You may not notice it right away, but two things happened. 1) Your incredible message was displayed in the Slack app, and 2) your Slack API app is now integrated into the Slack app.

Cool Idea 💡 : You can use Postman and Insomnia API clients to test and prototype your Slack webhooks and AWS Function URLs. ;)

You can stop here and call it a day, but continue the next section if you want to flex.

Create an AWS Lambda Function URL

Right then!

Let's take this tutorial to the next level. In this next section, I will show you how to create and configure an AWS Lambda Function URL so you can use the Slack Incoming Webhooks.😃

Also, we're going to use the AWS command-line interface to create AWS identity permission called roles and policies to allow the function URL to work.

If you need a refresher, visit the AWS IAM docs here.

Ready!? Let's go!

Create AWS IAM Role

First, we need to access your ARN, which is AWS specific formatted string. Similar to this one:

arn:aws:iam::123456789123:user/username

And to access that, we can execute the following AWS CLI command in the terminal like so:

Note: Change Paulo to your user name

$ aws iam get-user --user-name Paulo

The response should look similar to the JSON text below. Save your text somewhere to reference it later, or you can rerun the command.

$ { "User": { "UserName": "Paulo", "Path": "/", "CreateDate": "2019-09-21T23:03:13Z", "UserId": "AIDA123456789EXAMPLE", "Arn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/Paulo" }
}

Attach AWS Policy to IAM Role

Next, create a file called trust-policy.json

// trust-policy.json
{ "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "Service": "lambda.amazonaws.com" }, "Action": "sts:AssumeRole" } ]
}

Pause here and change the role name function-url-role to something else or keep it until you create another one.

Once you're ready, open your terminal or command prompt, copy-paste the command below, and press enter.

$ aws iam create-role --role-name function-url-role --assume-role-policy-document file://trust-policy.json

A successful response will return some more JSON text.

This next step will attach an AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole policy to your newly created IAM role. This policy gives you the basic user permissions to run your function.

Go ahead and copy-paste and execute the command below into your terminal app.

Remember to replace your function role name with yours. In this example its function-url-role.

$ aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name function-url-role --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole

Create AWS Function URL

In this section, you will create and configure your AWS Lambda Function and enable the new function URL feature with some basic settings.

First, we need access to the ARN role string. To do that, execute the AWS CLI command below.

$ aws iam get-role --role-name function-url-role

The response output should return some JSON similar to the text below.

Pause for a second and locate your the "Arn" string value which similar to this text arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/my-function-url and save it somewhere for later.

// terminal JSON ouput
{ "Role": { "Path": "/", "RoleName": "my-function-url", "RoleId": "AIDA123456789EXAMPLE", "Arn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/my-function-url", "CreateDate": "2022-04-16T22:32:36+00:00", "AssumeRolePolicyDocument": { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "Service": "lambda.amazonaws.com" }, "Action": "sts:AssumeRole" } ] }, "Description": "Allows Lambda functions to call AWS services on your behalf.", "MaxSessionDuration": 3600, "RoleLastUsed": { "LastUsedDate": "2022-04-30T00:29:38+00:00", "Region": "us-west-2" } }
}

Prepare Your Files

Before creating an AWS Lambda Function, you need to prepare some code, execute a few AWS CLI commands and finally deploy your project for live testing. For this example, we're going to use JavaScript for Node.js.

First, create a file called index.js, then prepare the file for editing. Use the command below with your preferred terminal app, or you can manually modify it.

$ touch index.js && vim index.js

Next, copy-paste the following JavaScript code into the vim edit session in the terminal or use your preferred code editor. Make sure to replace the "string" where it says <-- YOUR WEBHOOK GOES HERE --> with your Slack API webhook URL.

For example, from this:

path: '<-- YOUR WEBHOOK GOES HERE -->'

To this

path: '/services/T02S60H1F0Q/B03DH5L14S1/CPHv2eu0ctGgB9g99DNZiNCi'

// index.js
const https = require('https'); const postRequest = (a) => { const payload = { "text": `${a}` }; const data = JSON.stringify(payload); const options = { hostname: 'hooks.slack.com', path: '<-- YOUR WEBHOOK GOES HERE -->', port: 443, method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded; charset=UTF-8', 'Content-Length': data.length } }; return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { const req = https.request(options, res => { let rawData = ''; res.on('data', chunk => { rawData += chunk; }); res.on('end', () => { resolve({ statusCode: 200, body: `${rawData}` }); }); }); req.on('error', err => { reject(new Error(err)); }); req.write(data); req.end(); });
}; exports.handler = async (event) => { let body = JSON.parse(event.body); const result = await postRequest(body.text); const response = { statusCode: 200, body: result, }; return response;
};

Finally, zip your files using the command below or manually.

$ zip function.zip index.js

You create an AWS Lambda Function using the AWS CLI in the following sections.

Create AWS Lambda Function

Pause for a second here. Think of a name for your new function. In this example, I'm using my-url-function.

Remember that ARN string you saved earlier? Replace the ARN string with yours.

For example, from this:

--role [YOUR ARN GOES HERE]

To this

--role arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/my-function-url

When you're ready, copy-paste and execute the following command in the terminal:

$ aws lambda create-function \ --function-name my-url-function \ --runtime nodejs14.x \ --zip-file fileb://function.zip \ --handler index.handler \ --role [YOUR ARN GOES HERE]

The output should return an extended JSON response with your AWS Lambda Function details.

You can verify if your lambda function was created by using the following command in the terminal.

$ awd lambda get-functionn --function-name my-url-function

Enable AWS Function URL

Traditionally, you would have to go through more than a dozen steps to create a simple endpoint using AWS Lambda, API Gateway, and rightfully so when creating secure endpoints, but now there's a better way.

Execute the following command, and remember to replace your function name with yours.

From this:

--function-name YOUR-FUNCTION-NAME \

To this:

--function-name my-url-function \

$ aws lambda create-function-url-config \ --function-name YOUR-FUNCTION-NAME \ --auth-type NONE \ --cors '{"AllowOrigins": ["*"],"AllowCredentials": false}'

Note: For this demo, the link is public. You're responsible for securing your endpoints in all of your production environments.

Test Your AWS Function URL

Now for the moment you've been waiting for. Use the following command in your terminal and execute.

$ curl -X POST \ '<YOUR FUNCTION URL GOES HERE>' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -d '{"text": "Feed me Seymour!"}'

A successful response should return some JSON text similar to the one below.

$ {"body":"ok","statusCode":200}

If you are successful, you should see another message in the Slack app channel with the text "Feed me, Seymour!".

Conclusion

AND THAT'S IT!

I hope you leveled up some of your AWS Cloud Serverless skills and enjoyed this tutorial.

I appreciate you getting this far, and I hope to see you next time!
item.author: josuebustos

item.title: 使用 CSS 为元素设置动画 | Animate an Element Using CSS
item.description:
这些东西很强大。 您不需要 Javascript 库和复杂的专业知识即可将整洁的动画添加到您的普通网站。

只需一点点工作就可以为您的开发人员工具包的步骤增添一些活力。

这种小小的接触对Jen 的午餐钱应用程序大有帮助。

在听了 Jen 的 Indie Hacker 播客采访后，我在 2020 年初看到了这部动画，从那以后它就一直困扰着我。

现在，当谈到细节时，我有点古怪。 但我认为这一点仍然有效：动画（使用得当）吸引注意力并产生影响。

让元素旋转💫

我有一个小用例——真的是好奇——我想在其中进行<div>旋转。

够简单吧？ 但也很抢眼吧！？

的HTML

让我们来吧。 为了简单起见，我只是使用了一个 div 。 您可以对任何您想要的元素执行此操作。

你只受限于你的想象力+你的毅力。

我想要一个圆圈，所以：

 .circle{ border-radius: 50%; height: 200px; width: 200px; }

我想看看它在旋转，所以我将添加一个虚线边框：

 .circle{ border-radius: 50%; height: 200px; width: 200px; border: dashed white thick;

然后为了结束它，我们将添加动画属性：

 .circle{ border-radius: 50%; height: 200px; width: 200px; color: white; border: dashed white thick; animation: spinner 2s infinite linear; }

好的，不过，这还不会做任何事情。 因为我们需要使用一些keyframes来引导动画属性👇。

关键帧将指定在不同时间点需要发生的事情。

如果你再看一下Lunchmoney 的例子，硬币从顶部开始，然后下跌，然后反弹回顶部。

想想关键帧，比如时间的关键点。 当时间为零（或 0%）时，小硬币图标位于顶部。 当时间到一半时（50%），它在底部。 然后当循环结束 (100%) 时，它又回到顶部并准备再次下降。

我们所要做的就是指示 CSS 进行一次迭代，然后继续重复一段时间。 看一下这个：

 @keyframes spinner{ 0% {background-color: #3B82F6;} 50% {background-color:#1E293B;transform:rotate(180deg);} 100% {background-color: #3B82F6;transform:rotate(360deg);} }

我们声明关键帧（ @keyframes ），命名它（ spinner ），然后让它知道它在时间点（ 0%,50%,100% ）需要做什么。

在上面的旋转圆圈示例中，我将背景颜色从一种蓝色更改为另一种并返回，以及从 0 度旋转到 180 度再到 360 度。

现在 1 次迭代的方向已经到位，再看一下我们的 css 类的动画线：

 .circle{ animation: spinner 2s infinite linear; }

我们的 CSS 现在知道使用名为 spinner 的关键帧。 我们还告诉了它一些其他重要的事情。 我们指示它在 2 秒内完成从 0% 到 100% 的关键帧迭代。 对于我们的圈子，我们不希望它围绕过渡点（0%、50%、100% 点）旋转得更快或更慢。

还有很多其他的选项可供查看，并且像往常一样， MDN是一个不错的选择。

如果您将时间设为 10 秒而不是 2 秒，然后将线性更改为缓入缓出，则会发生以下情况：

 .spinner2{ animation: spinner2 10s infinite ease-in-out; }

它总体上变慢了，因为整个旋转现在需要 10 秒才能完成。 它也开始缓慢，慢慢进入“全速”，然后在每次旋转结束时减速。

谢谢！ 我希望这对你有帮助。 如果使用得当，CSS 动画可以为页面增光添彩。 让我知道您的想法，以及您如何在项目中使用动画。 在下方发表评论，或在Twitter 上与我联系！ 👋 👋

https://media.giphy.com/media/l3q2wJsC23ikJg9xe/giphy.gif https://media.giphy.com/media/l3q2wJsC23ikJg9xe/giphy.gif



原文:

These things are powerful. You don't need Javascript libraries and complicated know-how to add neat animations to your plain ol' website.

Just a little bit of work can put some pep into the step of your developer toolkit.

This small touch goes a long way on Jen's Lunch Money app.

https://media.giphy.com/media/UuQ2thBL0UCOJUcKSY/giphy.gif

I saw this animation back in early 2020 after listening to an Indie Hacker podcast interview with Jen, and it's stuck with me ever since.

Now, I'm a bit on the quirky side when it comes to details. But I think the point is still valid: animations (used well) draw attention and make an impact.

Make an Element Spin 💫

I have a small use case--a curiosity, really--where I wanted to make a <div> spin.

Simple enough, eh? But also pretty eye-catching, right!?

The HTML

Let's make it. I just used a div for simplicity's sake. You can do this to whatever element you want.

The CSS Class 🖼

You're only limited by your imagination + your perseverance.

I want a circle, so:

.circle{
    border-radius: 50%;
    height: 200px;
    width: 200px;
}

I want to see that it's spinning, so I'll add a dashed border:

.circle{
    border-radius: 50%;
    height: 200px;
    width: 200px;
    border: dashed white thick;

Then to cap it off, we'll add the animation property:

.circle{
    border-radius: 50%;
    height: 200px;
    width: 200px;
    color: white;
    border: dashed white thick;
    animation: spinner 2s infinite linear;
}

Okay, that's not going to do anything yet, though. Because we need to direct that animation property with some keyframes 👇.

The CSS Keyframes 🎬

Keyframes are going to specify what needs to be happening at different points in time.

If you look at the Lunchmoney example again, the coin starts out at the top, it goes down, and it bounces back to the top.

Think about the keyframes like key points in time. When time is zero (or 0%), the little coin icon is at the top. When time is halfway (50%), it's at the bottom. And then when the loop is concluded (100%), it's back at the top and ready to plunge down again.

All we have to do is instruct the CSS to go through one iteration, and then keep repeating for a certain amount of time. (In our case, that's for all eternity😂).

Check this out:

@keyframes spinner{
  0% {background-color: #3B82F6;}
  50% {background-color:#1E293B;transform:rotate(180deg);}
  100% {background-color: #3B82F6;transform:rotate(360deg);}
}

We declare the keyframe (@keyframes), we name it (spinner), and then we let it know what it needs to do at points in time (0%,50%,100%).

In my example above for the spinning circle, I'm changing the background-color from one blue to another and back as well as rotating from 0deg to 180deg to 360deg.

Now that the directions for 1 iteration are in place, take another look at the animation line of our css class:

.circle{
    animation: spinner 2s infinite linear;
}

Our CSS now knows to use the keyframes named spinner. We've also told it some other important things. 2s is of course 2 seconds. We're instructing it to go through the keyframe iteration from 0% to 100% over the course of 2 seconds. infinite lets it know to keep doing it forever.

And linear lets it know to keep a constant rate of change. For our circle, we don't want it spinning faster or slower around the transition points (the 0%,50%,100% points).

There are a ton of other options to check out, and as per usual, MDN is the place to go.

Here's what happens if you make the time 10s instead of 2s and then change linear to ease-in-out:

.spinner2{
    animation: spinner2 10s infinite ease-in-out;
}

It slows down overall because the whole rotation now takes 10 seconds to complete. And it also starts slow, eases into "full-speed" and then slows back down at the end of each rotation.

Thanks! 🙏

I hope that was helpful for you. CSS animations can do a lot to spruce up a page if used well. Let me know what you think, and how you've used animations in your projects. Leave a comment down below, or connect with me over on Twitter! 👋

https://media.giphy.com/media/l3q2wJsC23ikJg9xe/giphy.gif
item.author: sieis

item.title: plusOne Javascript 中的整数数组 | plusOne Array of Integers in Javascript
item.description:
发表于将大整数加一并返回结果数字数组。

例子：

输入：digits = [1,2,3] 输出：[1,2,4] 解释：数组表示整数 123。加一得到 123 + 1 = 124。

因此，结果应该是 [1,2,4]。

输入：digits = [9] 输出：[1,0] 解释：数组表示整数 9。加一得到 9 + 1 = 10。

因此，结果应该是 [1,0]。

这是我的实现：

 function plusOne(digits: number[]): number[] { let n = digits.length; for(let i=n-1; i>=0; i--) { if(digits[i] < 9) { digits[i]++; return digits; } digits[i] = 0; } let newArr = Array.from({length: n+1}, () => 0); newArr[0] = 1; return newArr; };

如果您有更简化和更好的解决方案，请提供。

谢谢。

皮埃尔-亨利·索里亚✨ - Apr 8 4月8日



原文:

Vishwak ❤️ JS

Posted on Apr 30

Increment the large integer by one and return the resulting array of digits.

Example:

Input: digits = [1,2,3] Output: [1,2,4] Explanation: The array represents the integer 123. Incrementing by one gives 123 + 1 = 124.

Thus, the result should be [1,2,4].

Input: digits = [9] Output: [1,0] Explanation: The array represents the integer 9. Incrementing by one gives 9 + 1 = 10.

Thus, the result should be [1,0].

Here is the my implementation:

function plusOne(digits: number[]): number[] {
   let n = digits.length;
    for(let i=n-1; i>=0; i--) {
        if(digits[i] < 9) {
            digits[i]++; return digits;
        }
        digits[i] = 0;
    }

    let newArr = Array.from({length: n+1}, () => 0);
    newArr[0] = 1;
    return newArr;
};

Please provide if you have more simplified and better solution.

Thanks.

Pierre-Henry Soria ✨ - Apr 8
item.author: urstrulyvishwak

item.title: 让我们谈谈 DevOps | Let's talk about DevOps
item.description:
不幸的是，DevOps 是一个被误解的术语。 因此，首先您需要了解 DevOps 不是工具或职位。 这是一种共享的心态。

以这种心态工作的人都知道，例如，在孤岛中工作是行不通的，开发和运营必须按照精益和敏捷原则一起工作，以快速连续地开发软件。

这是关于以不同的方式思考、社交编码、小批量工作，然后再次努力交付最低限度的可行产品。 但这还不是全部，DevOps 要求以不同的方式组织，也许会投入精力使组织以不同的方式影响设计和衡量，因为最终你会得到你所衡量的。

那么，让我们来谈谈 Devops 不是什么：

  • 不仅仅是将开发和运营结合起来
  • 不是一个单独的团队
  • 不是工具
  • 没有一种尺寸适合所有人
  • 不仅仅是自动化

我们不“做” DevOps
我们“成为”DevOps



原文:

DevOps, unfortunately is a misunderstood term. So, first of all you need to understand that DevOps is not a tool or a job title. It is a shared mindset.

Who works with this kind of mindset knows, for example, that working in silos just doesn't work and Development and Operations must work together following lean and agile principles to devivering software in a rapid an continuos manner.

It's about think differently, social coding, working in small batchs and again put effort to delivery a minimum vaible product. DevOps is also about work differentently (TDD, BDD, CI and CD).

But this is not all, DevOps demand organize differently, perhaps put energy to make the organization impact the design and measure differently because in the end you get what you measure.

So, let's talk about what Devops is not:

  • Not simply combining development and operations
  • Not a separate team
  • Not a tool
  • Not one size fits all
  • Not just automation

DevOps is a cultural change in which development and operations engineers work together during entire development cycle.

We don't "do" DevOps
We "become" DevOps

item.title: 船长的房间 | Hackerrank Problem Solution With Explanation Hackerrank 问题解决方案与解释 | The Captain's Room | Hackerrank Problem Solution With Explanation
item.description:
酒店有无限数量的房间。

一个晴朗的日子，有限数量的游客来酒店住宿。 游客包括： → 船长。

船长被分配了一个单独的房间，其余的人每组分配一个房间。

该列表包含所有游客的房间号。 除船长房间外，房间号每组会出现K次。

阿南特先生需要你帮他找到船长的房间号。 您不知道游客总数或家庭团体总数。

你只知道K的值和房间号列表。

输入格式
第一行包含一个整数K ，即每组的大小。
第二行包含房间号列表的无序元素。

输出格式
输出船长的房间号。

样本输入5

样本输出
问题链接： https ://www.hackerrank.com/challenges/py-the-captains-room/problem

解决方案（Python3）

 if __name__ == '__main__': k = int(input()) rooms = list(map(int, input().split())) roomSet = set(rooms) roomSum = sum(rooms) roomSetSum = sum(roomSet) * k captiansRoom = (roomSetSum - roomSum) // (k - 1) print(captiansRoom)

解释

这背后的逻辑是简单的数学。 如果所有唯一元素都存在于数组中K次，那么它们的总和将是唯一元素的和 K 次。 但是有一个独特的元素只出现一次。 所以roomSetSum变量的值将大于给定数组元素的实际总和，比如说x 。

如果我们从不同的角度来看它，那么我们会发现我们想要的元素，假设它z只存在一次，但从数组中丢失了k-1次。 所以我们可以得出结论x只不过是z乘以k-1 。 现在我们要做的就是进行逆向工程，只需找到x （如果它包含所有唯一元素恰好k次的房间数组的所有元素的总和与所有元素的实际总和之间的差异） rooms 数组，即包含所有元素k次但单个元素只包含一次的数组）并将其除以k-1和瞧！ 我们得到了答案。

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Mar 15 3月15日

Mahmoud Harmouch - Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 14 4月14日



原文:

Mr. Anant Asankhya is the manager at the INFINITE hotel. The hotel has an infinite amount of rooms.

One fine day, a finite number of tourists come to stay at the hotel. The tourists consist of: → A Captain.

→ An unknown group of families consisting of members per group where K ≠ 1.

The Captain was given a separate room, and the rest were given one room per group.

Mr. Anant has an unordered list of randomly arranged room entries. The list consists of the room numbers for all of the tourists. The room numbers will appear K times per group except for the Captain's room.

Mr. Anant needs you to help him find the Captain's room number. The total number of tourists or the total number of groups of families is not known to you.

You only know the value of K and the room number list.

Input Format
The first line consists of an integer, K, the size of each group.
The second line contains the unordered elements of the room number list.

Output Format
Output the Captain's room number.

Sample Input 5

1 2 3 6 5 4 4 2 5 3 6 1 6 5 3 2 4 1 2 5 1 4 3 6 8 4 3 1 5 6 2

Sample Output
8

Link to question: https://www.hackerrank.com/challenges/py-the-captains-room/problem

Solution (Python3) 

if __name__ == '__main__':
    k = int(input())
    rooms = list(map(int, input().split()))
    roomSet = set(rooms)
    roomSum = sum(rooms)
    roomSetSum = sum(roomSet) * k
    captiansRoom = (roomSetSum - roomSum) // (k - 1)
    print(captiansRoom)

Explanation

The logic behind this is simple mathematics. If all the unique elements were present in the array K times then their sum would have been sum of unique elements mutliplied K times. But there is one unique element that is present only one time. So the roomSetSum variable's value will be more than the actual sum of elements of the given array, let's say it x.

If we see it from a different angle then we will find that our desired element, let's say it z is present only one time but is missing k-1 times from the array. So we can conclude that x is nothing but z multiplied by k-1. Now all we've to do is to do reverse engineering and just find the x (that is the difference between the sum of all the elements of rooms array if it contained all the unique elements exactly k times and actual sum of all the elements of the rooms array, i.e. the array that contains all the elements k times but a single element only one time) and divide it with k-1 and voila! We got the answer.

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Mar 15

Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 14

item.title: 开源冒险：第 49 集：BATTLETECH 每吨武器全伤害排名 | Open Source Adventures: Episode 49: BATTLETECH Full Damage Per Ton Weapon Ranking
item.description:
托马斯·韦格扎诺夫斯基

发表于是时候完成项目，获得完整的武器排名了。 我们需要做的就是增加弹药重量。 我假设您将获得足够的弹药来进行 10 发射击，并且不进行任何四舍五入（即使您实际上无法获得 0.6 吨弹药）。

在此分析中，我还假设您想要在正常情况下获得足够的散热器以消除所有多余的热量。 这通常不是正确的构建方式，因为您不会一直在拍摄，所以 100% 散热只是浪费。

作为奖励，我还重新运行了这个脚本，假设您可以使用双散热器而不是单散热器。 这也可以被认为是一个 50% 的散热结构，这有点太低了。

我有点想知道将其变成交互式可视化，您可以在其中更改假设以根据您的选择获得不同的排名，例如您想要多少弹药，敌人掩护（20％实际上并不意味着20％，因为游戏方式四舍五入），任何热交换器，你对暴击加成或稳定性伤害的重视程度等。但现在我想我就停在这里了。

脚本

#!/usr/bin/env ruby require "json" require "memoist" require "pathname" require "pry" class AmmoBox extend Memoist attr_reader :data , :path def initialize ( path ) @path = path @data = JSON . parse ( path . read ) end memoize def id @data [ "AmmoID" ]. sub ( /\AAmmunition_/ , "" ) end def tonnage @data [ "Tonnage" ] end def capacity @data [ "Capacity" ] end end class Weapon extend Memoist attr_reader :game , :data , :path def initialize ( game , path ) @game = game @path = path @data = JSON . parse ( path . read ) end memoize def name bonuses = [ bonus_a , bonus_b ]. compact if bonuses . empty? base_name else " #{ base_name } ( #{ bonuses . join ( ", " ) } )" end end memoize def base_name [ data [ "Description" ][ "Name" ], data [ "Description" ][ "UIName" ], ]. compact . last . gsub ( " +" , "+" ) end memoize def bonus_a data [ "BonusValueA" ] == "" ? nil : data [ "BonusValueA" ]. gsub ( /[az]\K\./ , "" ) end memoize def bonus_b data [ "BonusValueB" ] == "" ? nil : data [ "BonusValueB" ]. gsub ( /[az]\K\./ , "" ) end def category @data [ "Category" ] end def subtype @data [ "WeaponSubType" ] end def tonnage @data [ "Tonnage" ] end def damage shots * base_damage end def base_damage @data [ "Damage" ] end def shots @data [ "ShotsWhenFired" ] end def heat @data [ "HeatGenerated" ] end def ammo_per_shot @data [ "ShotsWhenFired" ] * data [ "ProjectilesPerShot" ] end def heat_tonnage heat / 3.0 end # 10 rounds of shootnig at target def ammo_tonnage @game . ammo_weights . fetch ( ammo_category ) * ammo_per_shot * 10 end def total_tonnage tonnage + heat_tonnage + ammo_tonnage end def ammo_category @data [ "ammoCategoryID" ] || @data [ "AmmoCategory" ] end def purchasable? @data [ "Description" ][ "Purchasable" ] end def weapon_effect @data [ "WeaponEffectID" ] end def ignore? [ category == "Melee" , name == "AI Laser" , subtype == "TAG" , subtype == "Narc" , subtype =~ /\ACOIL/ , weapon_effect == "WeaponEffect-Artillery_MechMortar" , weapon_effect == "WeaponEffect-Artillery_Thumper" , ]. any? end def score [ damage . to_f / total_tonnage , damage , name ] end end class BattleTechGame extend Memoist def initialize ( game_root , * dlc_roots ) @game_root = Pathname ( game_root ) @dlc_roots = dlc_roots . map { | path | Pathname ( path )} end memoize def data_root @game_root + "BattleTech_Data/StreamingAssets/data" end def roots [ data_root , * @dlc_roots ] end memoize def weapon_files roots . flat_map { | root | root . glob ( "weapon/*.json" )} . select { | n | n . basename . to_s != "WeaponTemplate.json" } end memoize def weapons weapon_files . map { | path | Weapon . new ( self , path )} end memoize def ammobox_files roots . flat_map { | root | root . glob ( "ammunitionBox/*.json" )} . select { | n | n . basename . to_s != "AmmoBoxTemplate.json" } end memoize def ammoboxes ammobox_files . map { | path | AmmoBox . new ( path )} end memoize def ammo_weights # MG box occurs twice, but with same ratio ammoboxes . to_h { | a | [ a . id , a . tonnage . to_f / a . capacity ]}. merge ( "NotSet" => 0.0 ) end def inspect "BattechGame" end def print_weapons_report weapons . reject ( & :ignore? ) . sort_by ( & :score ) . reverse . each { | w | subtonage = [ w . tonnage > 0 ? " #{ w . tonnage } B" : nil , w . heat_tonnage > 0 ? " #{ w . heat_tonnage . round ( 2 ) } H" : nil , w . ammo_tonnage > 0 ? " #{ w . ammo_tonnage . round ( 2 ) } A" : nil , ]. compact . join ( " + " ) puts "* #{ w . name } : #{ w . damage } / #{ w . total_tonnage . round ( 2 ) } [ #{ subtonage } ]" } end end game = BattleTechGame . new ( * ARGV ) game . print_weapons_report

排行-单散热片

  • 冷落 PPC++ (+ 10 Dmg): 125 / 17.67 [6B + 11.67H]
  • 冷落 PPC+ (+ 5 Dmg): 100 / 17.67 [6B + 11.67H]
  • 冷落 PPC++（- 10 热度）：75 / 14.33 [6B + 8.33H]
  • 高斯步枪++（- 2 吨，- 1 个插槽）：75 / 15.92 [13B + 1.67H + 1.25A]
  • 高斯步枪+（- 2 吨）：75 / 15.92 [13B + 1.67H + 1.25A]
  • 冷落 PPC++ (+ 10 StbDmg): 75 / 17.67 [6B + 11.67H]
  • 冷落 PPC+ (+ 5 StbDmg): 75 / 17.67 [6B + 11.67H]
  • 冷落 PPC：75 / 17.67 [6B + 11.67H]
  • 高斯步枪：75 / 17.92 [15B + 1.67H + 1.25A]
  • 火焰喷射器+（* 4 弹药，+ 5 伤害）：10 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]
  • 地狱火（* 8 发球）：4 / 5.25 [3B + 1.0H + 1.25A]
  • 火焰喷射器+++（* 40 弹药）：5 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]
  • 火焰喷射器（* 4 弹药）：5 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]

排名——双散热片（或50%散热片）

  • 冷落 PPC++ (+ 10 Dmg): 125 / 11.83 [6B + 5.83H]
  • 冷落 PPC+ (+ 5 Dmg): 100 / 11.83 [6B + 5.83H]
  • 冷落 PPC++（- 10 热量）：75 / 10.17 [6B + 4.17H]
  • 冷落 PPC++ (+ 10 StbDmg): 75 / 11.83 [6B + 5.83H]
  • 冷落 PPC+ (+ 5 StbDmg): 75 / 11.83 [6B + 5.83H]
  • 冷落 PPC：75 / 11.83 [6B + 5.83H]
  • 高斯步枪++（- 2 吨，- 1 个插槽）：75 / 15.08 [13B + 0.83H + 1.25A]
  • 高斯步枪+（- 2 吨）：75 / 15.08 [13B + 0.83H + 1.25A]
  • 高斯步枪：75 / 17.08 [15B + 0.83H + 1.25A]
  • 火焰喷射器+（* 4 弹药，+ 5 伤害）：10 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]
  • 地狱火（* 8 发球）：4 / 4.75 [3B + 0.5H + 1.25A]
  • 火焰喷射器+++（* 40 弹药）：5 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]
  • 火焰喷射器（* 4 弹药）：5 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]

结论

这些是很长的列表，但我们可以从这些排名中看出：

  • 支援武器（MG和SL）非常好，但它们的射程非常低，所以它们只能用于特定的构建
  • 我认为这非常符合我的预期，因为我的构建已经是 SRM、ML、LRM，偶尔还会有 UAC、LBX 或 LL。 我想我预计高斯步枪会更好。

    所以最后我不确定我从中学到了多少。

    到目前为止的故事

    所有代码都在 GitHub 上。



原文:

Tomasz Wegrzanowski

Posted on Apr 30

It's time to complete the project, and get the full weapon ranking. All we need to do is add ammo weight. I'm assuming that you'll get enough ammo to last you 10 rounds of shooting, and not doing any rounding (even though you can't really get like 0.6 ton of ammo).

In this analysis I'm also assuming you want to get enough heat sinks to get rid of all the excess heat, under normal conditions. This is generally not the right way to build, as you won't be shooting all the time, so 100% heat siking is just a waste.

As a bonus I also reran this script assuming you have access to double heat sinks instead of single heat sinks. This could alternatively be taken to be a 50% heat sinking build, which would be a bit too low.

I'm sort of wondering about turning this into interactive visualization, where you can change your assumptions to get different rankings based on your choices, like how much ammo you want, enemy cover (20% does not actualyl mean 20% due to how game rounding works), any heat exchangers, how much you value crit bonus or stability damage etc. But for now I think I'll just stop here.

Script 

#!/usr/bin/env ruby require "json"
require "memoist"
require "pathname"
require "pry" class AmmoBox extend Memoist attr_reader :data, :path def initialize(path) @path = path @data = JSON.parse(path.read) end memoize def id @data["AmmoID"].sub(/\AAmmunition_/, "") end def tonnage @data["Tonnage"] end def capacity @data["Capacity"] end
end class Weapon extend Memoist attr_reader :game, :data, :path def initialize(game, path) @game = game @path = path @data = JSON.parse(path.read) end memoize def name bonuses = [bonus_a, bonus_b].compact if bonuses.empty? base_name else "#{base_name} (#{bonuses.join(", ")})" end end memoize def base_name [ data["Description"]["Name"], data["Description"]["UIName"], ].compact.last.gsub(" +", "+") end memoize def bonus_a data["BonusValueA"] == "" ? nil : data["BonusValueA"].gsub(/[a-z]\K\./, "") end memoize def bonus_b data["BonusValueB"] == "" ? nil : data["BonusValueB"].gsub(/[a-z]\K\./, "") end def category @data["Category"] end def subtype @data["WeaponSubType"] end def tonnage @data["Tonnage"] end def damage shots * base_damage end def base_damage @data["Damage"] end def shots @data["ShotsWhenFired"] end def heat @data["HeatGenerated"] end def ammo_per_shot @data["ShotsWhenFired"] * data["ProjectilesPerShot"] end def heat_tonnage heat / 3.0 end # 10 rounds of shootnig at target def ammo_tonnage @game.ammo_weights.fetch(ammo_category) * ammo_per_shot * 10 end def total_tonnage tonnage + heat_tonnage + ammo_tonnage end def ammo_category @data["ammoCategoryID"] || @data["AmmoCategory"] end def purchasable? @data["Description"]["Purchasable"] end def weapon_effect @data["WeaponEffectID"] end def ignore? [ category == "Melee", name == "AI Laser", subtype == "TAG", subtype == "Narc", subtype =~ /\ACOIL/, weapon_effect == "WeaponEffect-Artillery_MechMortar", weapon_effect == "WeaponEffect-Artillery_Thumper", ].any? end def score [damage.to_f / total_tonnage, damage, name] end
end class BattleTechGame extend Memoist def initialize(game_root, *dlc_roots) @game_root = Pathname(game_root) @dlc_roots = dlc_roots.map{|path| Pathname(path)} end memoize def data_root @game_root + "BattleTech_Data/StreamingAssets/data" end def roots [data_root, *@dlc_roots] end memoize def weapon_files roots .flat_map{|root| root.glob("weapon/*.json")} .select{|n| n.basename.to_s != "WeaponTemplate.json"} end memoize def weapons weapon_files.map{|path| Weapon.new(self, path)} end memoize def ammobox_files roots .flat_map{|root| root.glob("ammunitionBox/*.json")} .select{|n| n.basename.to_s != "AmmoBoxTemplate.json"} end memoize def ammoboxes ammobox_files.map{|path| AmmoBox.new(path)} end memoize def ammo_weights # MG box occurs twice, but with same ratio ammoboxes.to_h{|a| [a.id, a.tonnage.to_f/a.capacity]}.merge("NotSet" => 0.0) end def inspect "BattechGame" end def print_weapons_report weapons .reject(&:ignore?) .sort_by(&:score) .reverse .each{|w| subtonage = [ w.tonnage > 0 ? "#{w.tonnage}B" : nil, w.heat_tonnage > 0 ? "#{w.heat_tonnage.round(2)}H" : nil, w.ammo_tonnage > 0 ? "#{w.ammo_tonnage.round(2)}A" : nil, ].compact.join(" + ") puts "* #{w.name}: #{w.damage} / #{w.total_tonnage.round(2)} [#{subtonage}]" } end
end game = BattleTechGame.new(*ARGV)
game.print_weapons_report

Ranking - single heat sinks

  • MG++ (+ 5 Shots, - 0.5 Ton): 30 / 0.5 [0.5A]
  • MG++ (+ 50% Crit, - 0.5 Ton): 15 / 0.25 [0.25A]
  • MG+ (+ 5 Shots): 30 / 1.0 [0.5B + 0.5A]
  • MG+ (+ 25% Crit): 15 / 0.75 [0.5B + 0.25A]
  • MG: 15 / 0.75 [0.5B + 0.25A]
  • S Laser+++ (+ 10 Dmg, + 50% Crit): 30 / 2.5 [0.5B + 2.0H]
  • S Laser++ (+ 10 Dmg): 30 / 2.5 [0.5B + 2.0H]

  • Flamer+ (* 4 Ammo, + 5 Dmg): 10 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]
  • Infernos++ (* 8 Volleys, + 10 Dmg (H)): 4 / 4.75 [3B + 0.5H + 1.25A]
  • Infernos+ (* 8 Volleys, + 5 Dmg (H)): 4 / 4.75 [3B + 0.5H + 1.25A]
  • Infernos (* 8 Volleys): 4 / 4.75 [3B + 0.5H + 1.25A]
  • Flamer+++ (* 40 Ammo): 5 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]
  • Flamer++ (* 4 Ammo, + 5 Dmg (H)): 5 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]
  • Flamer (* 4 Ammo): 5 / 11.0 [1B + 10.0A]

Conclusions

These are long lists, but here's what we can tell from these rankings:

  • support weapons (MGs and SLs) are really good, but they have very low range, so they only go on specific builds
  • SRMs, MLs, LBXs, and UACs are good
  • LRMs are medium in the ranking, but they have very high range and indirect fire, so in
  • Snub PPCs and LLs are medium
  • ACs, PPCs, Gauss Rifles are quite poor

I think that's pretty much in line with what I expected, as my builds are already SRMs, MLs, LRMs, with occasional UAC, LBX, or LL. I guess I expected Gauss Rifles to be better.

So in the end I'm not sure I learned much from this.

Story so far

All the code is on GitHub.
item.author: taw

item.title: 机密计算联盟 - 下一代数据安全 | Confidential Computing Consortium- The next generation of Data Security
item.description:
很高兴回到另一个有趣的博客！

在之前的博客中，我们探讨了 WebAssembly 的属性和应用，

但是今天我们将了解 CCC（机密计算联盟）是一个由 Linux 基金会提供支持的开源社区，它为 Wasm 锦上添花。

机密计算通过在基于硬件的可信执行环境中执行计算来保护正在使用的数据。 这些安全且隔离的环境可防止在使用时对应用程序和数据进行未经授权的访问或修改，从而提高管理敏感和受监管数据的组织的安全保障。

重要的是，该定义不仅限于“云”使用，还可以应用于任何地方，包括公共云服务器、本地服务器、网关、物联网设备、边缘部署、用户设备等。它也不限于此类可信执行由任何特定处理器完成，因为可信处理也可能在其他各种地方，例如 GPU 或网络接口卡。 它也不限于使用加密的解决方案，尽管这是最常用的技术。

可信执行环境 (TEE)：

可信执行环境 (TEE) 由 CCC 定义，遵循常见的行业惯例，作为对以下三个属性提供一定程度保证的环境： 数据机密性：未经授权的实体无法查看 TEE 中使用的数据。 湾。 数据完整性：未经授权的实体不能在 TEE 中使用数据时添加、删除或更改数据。

代码完整性：未经授权的实体不能添加、删除或更改在 TEE 中执行的代码。

在机密计算的背景下，未经授权的实体可能包括主机上的其他应用程序、主机操作系统和管理程序、系统管理员、服务提供商和基础设施所有者——或任何其他对硬件具有物理访问权限的人。

将受益于 CC 的技术：

它们如下：

  1. 埃纳克斯
  2. 禾本科
  3. 梯形
  4. 遮盖物
  5. 打开 Enclave SDK
  6. 韦拉克鲁斯

参与该计划的组织：

机密计算的总体预期市场增长：

  1. 由于企业对机密计算的认识不断提高，在最佳情况下，CC 的总可寻址市场 (TAM) 可能在未来五年内至少增长 26 倍。
  2. 在不断扩大的法规、对全面安全态势的需求以及对新收入来源的需求的支持下，CC 硬件和软件市场有望实现指数级增长。
  3. 跨行业的多方计算和特定于安全的用例将成为该技术的最大增长动力。

结论：

结果，由于技术的日益复杂，隐私和安全变得更加难以保护。 机密计算联盟是一项伟大的倡议，致力于通过创建复杂的环境来确保任何系统中的数据安全。 让越来越多的贡献者参与进来，这将在短时间内迅速崛起。

请留意本系列的下一篇博客，我们将在其中了解组成机密计算联盟的各种项目。

参考：

请评论您与博客相关的想法和建议，如果您觉得有用，请分享。



原文:

It's good to be back with another interesting blog!

WASM is considered as the third generation of Cloud Computing, and it is expected to boom in the upcoming time therefore Data Security needs an upgrade to keep up.

In previous blogs, we explored the properties & applications of WebAssembly,

But today we're going to learn about CCC (Confidential Computing Consortium) is an open-source community powered by The Linux Foundation, which works as the icing on the cake for Wasm.

Confidential Computing protects data in use by performing the computation in a hardware-based Trusted Execution Environment. These secure and isolated environments prevent unauthorized access or modification of applications and data while in use, thereby increasing the security assurances for organizations that manage sensitive and regulated data.

Importantly, the definition is not limited to “cloud” uses, but can be applied anywhere including public cloud servers, on-premises servers, gateways, IoT devices, Edge deployments, user devices, etc. It is also not limited to such trusted execution being done by any particular processor, since trusted processing might also be in various other places such as a GPU or a network interface card. Neither is it limited to solutions that use encryption, though this is the most common technique employed.

Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs):

A Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) is defined by the CCC, following common industry practice, as an environment that provides a level of assurance of the following three properties: a. Data confidentiality: Unauthorized entities cannot view data while it is in use within the TEE. b. Data integrity: Unauthorized entities cannot add, remove, or alter data while it is in use within the TEE.

c. Code integrity: Unauthorized entities cannot add, remove, or alter code executing in the TEE.

In the context of confidential computing, unauthorized entities could include other applications on the host, the host operating system and hypervisor, system administrators, service providers, and the infrastructure owner—or anyone else with physical access to the hardware.

Technologies that will be benefited by CC:

There are several projects within CCC with the main focus on the security and privacy of digital data. They are as follows:

  1. Enarx
  2. Gramine
  3. Keystone
  4. Occlum
  5. Open Enclave SDK
  6. Veracruz

Organisations that are part of this initiative:

Overall Expected Market growth of Confidential Computing:

  1. The Total Addressable Market (TAM) for CC is likely to grow at least 26x over the next five years in the best-case scenario due to growing enterprise awareness of confidential computing.
  2. The CC hardware and software markets are poised for exponential growth backed by expanding regulations, demand for comprehensive security postures, and an appetite for new revenue generators.
  3. Multi-party computing and security-specific use cases across industries will be the biggest growth drivers for the technology.

Conclusion:

As a result, due to the increasing complexity of technology, privacy and security have become more difficult to protect. The Confidential Computing Consortium is a great initiative that is working to keep data secure in any system by creating a sophisticated enviornment. Getting more and more contributors in will put this on a meteoric rise within a short time.

Keep an eye out for the next blog in this series in which we will learn about the various projects that make up the Confidential Computing Consortium.

References:

Do comment your thoughts and suggestions related to the blog and please share if you found it useful.
item.author: aryank21

item.title: Entity Framework Core：使用 AutoMapper 简化查询 | Entity Framework Core: Simplify your queries with AutoMapper
item.description:
对于代码示例，我们将使用以下实体：

 public class Blog { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string Name { get ; set ; } public ICollection < Post > Posts { get ; set ; } } public class Post { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string Title { get ; set ; } public Blog Blog { get ; set ; } public List < Comment > Comments { get ; set ; } } public class Comment { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string Content { get ; set ; } public Post Post { get ; set ; } }

和 DTO：

 public class PostDto { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string Title { get ; set ; } public int BlogId { get ; set ; } public string BlogName { get ; set ; } public List < CommentDto > CommentsDto { get ; set ; } } public class CommentDto { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string Content { get ; set ; } } public class PostSummaryDto { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string Title { get ; set ; } public int CommentsCount { get ; set ; } }

当使用 Entity Framework 查询数据时，使用 Include 方法很常见。 但这可能有两个缺点：您将查询可能不需要的字段，第二个可能需要将您的实体映射到另一个模型或 DTO。

 var posts = await dbContext . Posts . Include ( x => x . Blog ) . Include ( x => x . Comments ) . ToListAsync (); // map to DTO

对于此查询，Entity Framework 将生成以下 SQL 代码：

 SELECT [ p ].[ Id ], [ p ].[ BlogId ], [ p ].[ Title ], [ b ].[ Id ], [ b ].[ Name ], [ c ].[ Id ], [ c ].[ Content ], [ c ].[ PostId ] FROM [ Posts ] AS [ p ] INNER JOIN [ Blogs ] AS [ b ] ON [ p ].[ BlogId ] = [ b ].[ Id ] LEFT JOIN [ Comments ] AS [ c ] ON [ p ].[ Id ] = [ c ].[ PostId ] ORDER BY [ p ].[ Id ], [ b ].[ Id ]

解决此问题的现有方法是使用投影，只需选择您需要的字段并直接使用 DTO 而不是实体：

 var post = await dbContext . Posts . Select ( x => new PostDto () { Id = x . Id , Title = x . Title , BlogId = x . Blog . Id , BlogName = x . Blog . Name , CommentsDto = x . Comments . Select ( c => new CommentDto () { Id = c . Id , }). ToList () }) . ToListAsync ();

使用以下查询作为结果：

 SELECT [ p ].[ Id ], [ p ].[ Title ], [ b ].[ Id ], [ b ].[ Name ], [ c ].[ Id ] FROM [ Posts ] AS [ p ] INNER JOIN [ Blogs ] AS [ b ] ON [ p ].[ BlogId ] = [ b ].[ Id ] LEFT JOIN [ Comments ] AS [ c ] ON [ p ].[ Id ] = [ c ].[ PostId ] ORDER BY [ p ].[ Id ], [ b ].[ Id ]

但是手动编写查询可能有点乏味。

生成与投影完全相同的查询：

 SELECT [ p ].[ Id ], [ p ].[ Title ], [ b ].[ Id ], [ b ].[ Name ], [ c ].[ Id ] FROM [ Posts ] AS [ p ] INNER JOIN [ Blogs ] AS [ b ] ON [ p ].[ BlogId ] = [ b ].[ Id ] LEFT JOIN [ Comments ] AS [ c ] ON [ p ].[ Id ] = [ c ].[ PostId ] ORDER BY [ p ].[ Id ], [ b ].[ Id ]

例如，我们可以有一个较小的 DTO，只返回要在前端列出的帖子摘要，只包含 id、名称和评论数：

 public class PostSummaryDto { public int Id { get ; set ; } public string Title { get ; set ; } public int CommentsCount { get ; set ; } }

并在 C# 上再次只写 1 行：

 var postSummary = await mapper . ProjectTo < PostSummaryDto >( dbContext . Posts , null ). ToListAsync ();

正如我们所见，我们只需使用 1 行代码就可以进行非常高效的查询。 如果您想了解更多关于.ProjectTo的信息，请在此处阅读。



原文:

For the examples of code, we are going to use the following entities:

public class Blog
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string Name { get; set; } public ICollection<Post> Posts { get; set; }
} public class Post
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string Title { get; set; } public Blog Blog { get; set; } public List<Comment> Comments { get; set; }
} public class Comment
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string Content { get; set; } public Post Post { get; set; }
}

And DTOs:

public class PostDto
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string Title { get; set; } public int BlogId { get; set; } public string BlogName { get; set; } public List<CommentDto> CommentsDto { get; set; }
} public class CommentDto
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string Content { get; set; }
} public class PostSummaryDto
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string Title { get; set; } public int CommentsCount { get; set; }
}

When querying data with Entity Framework is very common to use the Include method. But that can have two drawbacks: you will query fields that probably you don't need, and the second one maybe you will need to map your entities to another model or DTO.

var posts = await dbContext.Posts .Include(x => x.Blog) .Include(x => x.Comments) .ToListAsync(); // map to DTO

For this query Entity Framework will generate the following SQL code:

SELECT [p].[Id], [p].[BlogId], [p].[Title], [b].[Id], [b].[Name], [c].[Id], [c].[Content], [c].[PostId]
FROM [Posts] AS [p]
INNER JOIN [Blogs] AS [b] ON [p].[BlogId] = [b].[Id]
LEFT JOIN [Comments] AS [c] ON [p].[Id] = [c].[PostId]
ORDER BY [p].[Id], [b].[Id]

An existing way to solve this is using a projection, just picking the fields that you need and using directly the DTO instead of entities:

var post = await dbContext.Posts .Select(x => new PostDto() { Id = x.Id, Title = x.Title, BlogId = x.Blog.Id, BlogName = x.Blog.Name, CommentsDto = x.Comments.Select(c => new CommentDto() { Id = c.Id, }).ToList() }) .ToListAsync();

With the following query as result:

SELECT [p].[Id], [p].[Title], [b].[Id], [b].[Name], [c].[Id]
FROM [Posts] AS [p]
INNER JOIN [Blogs] AS [b] ON [p].[BlogId] = [b].[Id]
LEFT JOIN [Comments] AS [c] ON [p].[Id] = [c].[PostId]
ORDER BY [p].[Id], [b].[Id]

But manually writing the query can be a bit tedious.

ProjectTO to the rescue

AutoMapper has a simple way to solve this using the .ProjectTo method:

var posts = await mapper.ProjectTo<PostDto>(dbContext.Posts, null).ToListAsync();

Generating exactly the same query as the projection:

SELECT [p].[Id], [p].[Title], [b].[Id], [b].[Name], [c].[Id]
FROM [Posts] AS [p]
INNER JOIN [Blogs] AS [b] ON [p].[BlogId] = [b].[Id]
LEFT JOIN [Comments] AS [c] ON [p].[Id] = [c].[PostId]
ORDER BY [p].[Id], [b].[Id]

The .ProjectTo<PostDto>() will tell AutoMapper's mapping engine to emit a select clause to the IQueryable that will inform Entity Framework that it only needs to query the fields presents in the DTO.

A common case for the .ProjectTo is using small DTOs/models. For example, we can have a smaller DTO to just return a summary of the posts to list in the frontend, with just the id, name, and comments counts:

public class PostSummaryDto
{ public int Id { get; set; } public string Title { get; set; } public int CommentsCount { get; set; }
}

And writing again only 1 line on C#:

var postSummary = await mapper.ProjectTo<PostSummaryDto>(dbContext.Posts, null).ToListAsync();

The SQL code looks really nice:

SELECT [p].[Id], [p].[Title], ( SELECT COUNT(*) FROM [Comments] AS [c] WHERE [p].[Id] = [c].[PostId] ) AS [CommentsCount]
FROM [Posts] AS [p]

As we can see we can have a very performant query just using 1 line of code. If you want to read more about .ProjectTo please read here.

item.title: 哪个 Ide 最好？ | Which the best Ide ?
item.description:
亚历克斯马尔多纳多索托

发表于你有没有想过哪个是最好的 IDE？，有一个比所有其他 IDE 都大吗？ 在这里我有答案。

什么是 IDE？

要了解哪个是最好的 ide，您首先必须知道它的用处是什么。

开发者使用 ide 让我们更容易通过不同的工具或插件编写代码。 所以一个ide的插件越多越好吗？

最著名的代码编辑器 vscode 也是出于同样的原因而被认可，但是，还有许多其他 IDE 的环境更友好，并且受到其他开发人员的青睐。

适合所有人的“IDE”

需要注意的是，vscode 被许多开发人员列为 javascript 和 python 的最佳代码编辑器，但不适用于其他语言。

什么决定了“ide”的好坏？

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

AlexMaldonadoSoto

Posted on Apr 30

Have you ever wondered which is the best IDE?, there is one bigger than all other IDE? here I have the answer.

What is a IDE?

To understand which is the best ide, you first have to know what its usefulness is.

Developers use the ide to make it easier for us to write code through different tools or plugins. So the more plugins an ide has, the better it is?

vscode, the most famous code editor, is recognized for the same reason, however, there are many other IDE that have a more friendly environment and are preferred by other developers.

An "IDE" for everyone

It should be noted that vscode is cataloged by many developers as the best code editor for javascript and python, but not for other languages.

What determines how good an "ide" is?

Cristian Fernando - Apr 27

item.title: Swift 基础：常量和变量 | Swift Basic: Constants and Variable
item.description:
奥佩耶米·诺亚

发表于无论您选择何种编程方式，您都需要存储数据。

迅速有两种方法可以做到这一点，

什么是变量？

变量是可以随时更改其值的数据存储。

变量的值可以变化 - Paul Hudson
什么是常数？

常量是一个数据存储，一旦设置或指定它的值就不能改变。

常量具有恒定的值（不能更改）。
阿斯米特马拉卡纳瓦尔 - Apr 13 4月13日



原文:

Opeyemi Noah

Posted on Apr 30

A moment will come where you will need to store data, irrespective of the programming of your choice.

In swift there are two ways to do this ,

Whats a variable ?

Variable is a data store that can have its value changed anytime.

variable have value that can vary - Paul Hudson
"var" keyword is used to denote varibale

Whats a Constant ?

Constant is a data store that cant have it's value changed once it is set or specified.

constant have value that are constant (cannot be changed).
"let" keyword is ued to denote constant.

Asmit Malakannawar - Apr 13

item.title: API 101 使用邮递员 | API 101 USING POSTMAN
item.description:
大家好，欢迎来到自己的博客，Api 使用 Postman 的介绍。 我们将一起学习和探索更多关于 Api 使用 Postman 的信息。

让我们了解什么是 API？：通过将其视为数字餐厅来了解它。 它就像一个服务员，你的角色就像一个通过服务员订购某物的客户，它将请求发送到服务器并相应地给出输出。

现在以技术手段：

  • 应用程序编程接口 (API) 允许服务相互通信。
  • 多年来，该软件变得更加复杂和协作。 开发人员不再需要从头开始创建服务。
  • 有一个 API 适用于 cat API、OpenWeatherMap、Genius、Discord、Youtube-to-MP3 等所有内容。

现在，来到 postman API： Postman 是可扩展的 API 测试工具，可快速集成到 CI/CD 管道中。 它是一个协作 API 开发平台，可简化使用 UI 创建和测试 API。 使用邮递员处理 URL 变得更加容易，而不是通过命令行执行繁琐的任务。

这是邮递员的观点：



检索和更新 API 数据的要点：
 Methods, Addresses, Parameters, Authorization, Body Data

请求和响应：我们使用 3 个帮助我们发出请求的成分与 api 进行交互：

 Method (Get, Post, etc), Address/Endpoint(URL), Path


我们通过指定详细信息来构建我们的请求，例如：
响应元素


Status Codes(200 OK, 201 Created,404 Not Found), Headers, Accessing Body data
现在让我们做一些实际的实现： Joke API 的实现：- Copy Endpoint URL Paste On Get Data，查看输出：- Hello World！

默认情况下，我们有 4 个 id [0-3] 的笑话来获取随机笑话：{base_url}/joke

获取特定笑话：{base_url}/joke/{id of笑话}



这里是笑话 Api 的视图：-


此处查看更新的笑话 Api：-


删除（笑话）：-
删除笑话：{base_url}/joke/{id of joke}

这是删除后的视图：-


希望大家对api有一个基本的了解
太感谢了！！！
特别感谢 Ali Mustufa 先生

弗朗西斯科·莫雷蒂—— Apr 30 4月30日



原文:

Hello Folks, Welcome to your Own Blog, Introduction To Api's Using Postman. Together we will learn and explore more about Api's using Postman.

Let's Understand What's an API?: Understand it by Considering it as a digital restaurant. It acts like a waiter your role is like a client who orders Something via Waiter and it Sends the request to the Server and gives output Accordingly.

Now In Technical Means:

  • Application Programming Interface (APIs) allow Services to Communicate with each other.
  • The Software has become more complex and Collaborative over the years. Developer no Longer need to create service From scratch.
  • APIs allow developers to access data from a service (like Google or Twitter) without any knowledge of how the codebase has been implemented.
  • There is an API for everything like the cat API, OpenWeatherMap, Genius, Discord, Youtube-to-MP3, etc.

Now, Coming to postman API: Postman is scalable API testing tool that Quickly Integrates into CI/CD pipeline. It is collaborative API development platform that simplifies creating using, and testing APIs with a UI. It has become Much Easier to work with URLs using postman instead of doing tedious tasks via command lines.

Here Is the View Of Postman:



Essentials To Retrieve and Update API Data:
Methods, Addresses, Parameters, Authorization, Body Data

Request and Responses: We interact with api's using 3 ingredients that help us to make requests:

Method (Get, Post, etc), Address/Endpoint(URL), Path


We Build Our Requests By Specifying Details Like:
Parameters (In the Query), Authorization, Headers and Body (Data Types) So What is the Point even after doing a lot many things we don't get output, right?

Responses Elements


Status Codes(200 OK, 201 Created,404 Not Found), Headers, Accessing Body data
Now Let's Do Some Practical Implementation: Implementation Of Joke API:- Copy Endpoint URL Paste On Get Data, See Output:- Hello World!

GETs Method -

By default, we have 4 jokes with id [0-3] To get a random joke: {base_url}/joke

To get a specific joke: {base_url}/joke/{id of joke}



Post Method - { "id": "1", "author": "Ali Mustufa", "joke": "Bugs are features", "source": "iali.dev"

}

Here View Of Joke Api:-


Put (Update Joke) { "id": "1", "author": "Ali Mustufa", "joke": "Bugs are updated features", "source": "iali.dev"

}

Here View Of Updated Joke Api:-


Delete (Joke):-
To delete a joke: {base_url}/joke/{id of joke}

Here is The View After Delete:-


I Hope You All Got A Basic Understanding Of Api's
Thank You So Much!!!
Special Thanks To Ali Mustufa Sir

Francisco Moretti - Apr 30

item.title: 我的第一篇文章 | My first post
item.description:
我写在这里并不是为了真正吸引任何真正的读者，而是记录我作为一名自学成才的程序员的旅程。 我想保持我的帖子尽可能简洁和自发。 因为我倾向于过度思考事情并且不想浪费我们的时间。 考虑一下我的免责声明，因为我开始获得牵引力，我在这里输入的内容来自 19 年不到一年的经验。

话虽如此，我想回顾一下我对软件和编程的热爱：

  • 社区
  • 开源意识形态
  • 高杠杆技巧
  • 创造/创新能力
  • 抽象的思维方式

什瓦什·亚达夫—— Apr 30 4月30日



原文:

I write here with no intention to really garner any real audience but document my journey as a self-taught programmer. I'd like to keep my posts as concise and spontaneous as possible. As I tend to overthink things and do not want to waste either of our time. Consider this my disclaimer on the off chance I start to gain traction, that what I type here is coming from a 19 year with less than a year of experience.

With that said, I'd like to go over what I love about software and programming in general:

  • Community
  • Open source ideology
  • High leverage skill
  • Ability to create/innovate
  • Abstract way of thinking

Shivashish Yadav - Apr 30
